build.//buildCommand: Two reasons we use `npm install` instead of `npm ci`: (1) Railpack mounts /app/node_modules/.cache as a build cache volume — `npm ci` wipes node_modules entirely as step one and EBUSYs on rmdir of the mounted .cache. `npm install` leaves node_modules in place. (2) Peer-dep tolerance: @nestjs/apollo wants @nestjs/common ^11.0.1 while we track 11.1.x — handled by the .npmrc legacy-peer-deps=true in this directory.
build.buildCommand: npm install --no-audit && npx prisma generate && npm run build
build.//: watchPatterns are evaluated relative to the repo root. Limiting to HOA-API/** stops sibling-app changes (HOA-ENTERPRISE, HOA-RESIDENTS, HOA-MARKETING) from triggering an API redeploy.

deploy.//: Push schema before booting so a fresh deploy never crashes against an out-of-date DB. `db push --accept-data-loss` is the right call here because Prisma migrations aren't checked in — the schema file is the source of truth.
deploy.startCommand: npx prisma db push --skip-generate --accept-data-loss && node dist/main
